title: Disney+ VPN Proxy Error: Error Code 83, Error 73, and How to Fix Them
description: Disney+ throws error code 83 and error 73 when it detects a VPN or proxy. This article explains why Disney+ blocks VPNs so aggressively and which VPNs still bypass it in 2026.
content:
## The Errors: Disney+ Error Code 83 and Error Code 73

When Disney+ detects a VPN or proxy, it typically shows one of two error codes:

- **Error Code 83** - "Something went wrong. Please try again. If the problem persists visit the Disney+ Help Center (Error Code 83)."
- **Error Code 73** - "Disney+ is not available in your region."

Error 83 is more common and more confusing because it appears even when you are connecting from a VPN server in a country where Disney+ is available. It is not a geo-block. It is Disney+'s proxy detection triggering and refusing the connection.

Error 73 is the cleaner geo-block message, usually shown when Disney+ can identify that your server IP is from a country or region it does not serve.

## Why Disney+ Is the Most Aggressive Streaming Service for VPN Detection

Disney+ runs one of the most advanced VPN detection systems of any major streaming service. Several factors make it harder to bypass than Netflix or BBC iPlayer:

**Commercial IP reputation databases:** Disney+ subscribes to real-time feeds that flag IP ranges belonging to datacentres, hosting companies, and known VPN providers. If you share a VPN server with thousands of other users, the IP is almost certainly already on these lists. The moment you connect, the IP is recognized as proxy infrastructure.

**Traffic volume analysis:** When thousands of Disney+ subscribers connect from the same IP address simultaneously, the platform flags that IP. Cheap or popular VPNs burn through IP addresses quickly because their shared IPs get flagged within hours of being put into service.

**Device sensor data on mobile:** On Android and iOS, Disney+ can request GPS coordinates, Wi-Fi network region, device time zone, and system locale. If your VPN says "United States" but your phone's GPS reports Amsterdam, Disney+ triggers error 73 or 83 immediately. This is why VPNs that work on desktop sometimes fail on the Disney+ mobile app.

**Deep packet inspection (DPI):** Disney+ uses DPI to check whether your traffic looks like it came through a VPN tunnel. OpenVPN has recognizable packet timing and handshake patterns. WireGuard is harder to detect but some implementations are still fingerprinted. VPNs with obfuscation or stealth protocols evade this more reliably.

## Which VPNs Bypass Disney+ Error 83 in 2026

**NordVPN** is the most consistently successful option for Disney+. With 9,300+ servers across 137 countries, it maintains large enough IP pools per region that blocked servers are constantly replaced. NordVPN's obfuscated servers make traffic look like standard HTTPS, which helps bypass DPI detection. On mobile, make sure to also disable the Disney+ app's location permissions if you are seeing error 83 on iOS or Android specifically.

**Surfshark** bypasses Disney+ reliably. Its camouflage mode obscures VPN traffic fingerprints, and its server pool in the US and other major Disney+ markets is large enough that clean IPs are available.

**ExpressVPN** also works for Disney+. Its proprietary Lightway protocol is harder to fingerprint than standard OpenVPN.

Free VPNs and most budget VPNs consistently fail against Disney+ because their IP pools are too small and too heavily shared.

## Dedicated IP Addresses Are the Most Reliable Fix

The core reason shared VPN IPs get blocked is that Disney+ sees hundreds of concurrent connections from the same IP and marks it as a proxy. A dedicated IP is assigned exclusively to you. Disney+ never sees anomalous traffic volume from that IP, so it does not flag it.

NordVPN offers dedicated IP addresses as a paid add-on. You pick a country (US, UK, DE, etc.) and that IP is yours alone. Error 83 essentially disappears because your traffic pattern looks like a single legitimate subscriber, not a VPN proxy.

Surfshark also offers dedicated IPs. If you access Disney+ daily and error 83 keeps appearing with shared servers, a dedicated IP is the cleanest long-term solution.

## Step-by-Step Fix for Error Code 83

If you are already subscribed to a VPN and seeing error 83:

1. **Switch servers.** Do not give up on the first server. Try three or four servers in your target country. NordVPN and Surfshark both have many servers per country.
2. **Enable obfuscation.** In NordVPN: Settings > Advanced > Obfuscated Servers. In Surfshark: Settings > Protocol > set to Shadowsocks or select NoBorders mode.
3. **Switch protocol.** If you are using OpenVPN, switch to NordLynx (NordVPN) or WireGuard. These are harder to fingerprint.
4. **On mobile, revoke Disney+ location permissions.** On Android: Settings > Apps > Disney+ > Permissions > Location > Deny. On iOS: Settings > Privacy > Location Services > Disney+ > Never.
5. **Clear the Disney+ app cache.** On Android: Settings > Apps > Disney+ > Storage > Clear Cache. On iOS: delete and reinstall the app.
6. **Check for IPv6 leaks.** Visit ipleak.net while connected to your VPN. If any IPv6 addresses show your real location, disable IPv6 on your device or switch to a VPN that blocks IPv6 leaks.
7. **Try a browser instead of the app.** Disney+'s mobile app has access to more device sensors than the browser. If the browser version works but the app does not, device-level sensor data is causing the block.

## Recommendation

**NordVPN** is the best VPN for Disney+ in 2026. Its server pool is large enough to always have clean IPs available, obfuscated servers handle DPI, and its dedicated IP add-on eliminates error 83 permanently. **Surfshark** is the better value option if you need multiple devices covered simultaneously. Either one, combined with a dedicated IP, solves the proxy detection problem for Disney+ without ongoing server-switching frustration.
